metrics.label: Cadence
metrics.note: Personalized to your stature and speed: you're likely most efficient near 182 steps/min. Shorter runners and faster paces sit higher than the old '180' rule of thumb.

metrics.label: Trunk lean
metrics.note: A slight forward lean from the ankles (~5-12 deg) helps. Upright = braking; too far = back load.

metrics.label: Knee flexion (midstance)
metrics.note: ~40-50 deg of knee bend at midstance absorbs landing shock. Stiff knees jar the joints.

metrics.label: Overstride
metrics.note: Foot should land close to under your hips. Landing far ahead (>~8% of leg length) brakes you.

metrics.label: Hip extension (peak)
metrics.note: How far the thigh drives behind you at push-off. Limited extension often means tight hip flexors or under-active glutes — and a tendency to overstride to compensate.

metrics.label: Knee drive (peak)
metrics.note: How far the thigh swings forward in recovery. More knee drive feeds a longer, springier stride.

metrics.label: Vertical oscillation
metrics.note: Bouncing wastes energy. Lower vertical travel of the hips is generally more economical.

metrics.label: Ground contact time
metrics.note: Efficient runners spend ~200-250 ms on the ground per step. Long contact = less reactive.

metrics.label: Duty factor
metrics.note: Share of the stride your foot is on the ground. Lower is springier/faster (fps-limited estimate).

metrics.label: Elbow angle
metrics.note: A relaxed ~90° elbow. Very straight arms waste energy and tend to swing across the body.

metrics.label: Arm swing
metrics.note: Fore-aft arm drive. Aim for relaxed, even swing front-to-back (not across the body).

metrics.label: Heel recovery
metrics.note: How much the heel picks up in swing (proxy). More recovery = a shorter, springier swing leg.

metrics.label: Flight time
metrics.note: Time both feet are off the ground per step (fps-limited estimate).

metrics.label: Foot strike
metrics.note: Where your foot first contacts: heel, midfoot, or forefoot. None is inherently bad — it's the overstride that matters.

metrics.label: Vertical ratio
metrics.note: Bounce relative to step length — lower is more economical (~6-7% is good).

metrics.label: Stride length
metrics.note: From treadmill speed × stride time. Requires speed input.

metrics.label: Head bobbing
metrics.note: Vertical head-crown bounce per stride. Ideally tracks with hip VO; a much higher value suggests the head is nodding independently.

feedback.title: Solid mechanics
feedback.detail: No major flags in this clip — your cadence, alignment, and symmetry look within healthy ranges.
feedback.cue: Keep doing what you're doing; recheck periodically.
feedback.drill: Maintain your current routine and strength work.

quality.message: At 60 fps, ground-contact timing is approximate — use 120/240 fps slow-mo for it.
